title: Bosph (species)
content:
{{Top|fa|canon=Unidentified Bosph species}}
{{Youmay|the [[Species/Legends|species]]|the [[Bosph/Legends|planet]]}}

{{Quote|A true man of his word.|[[Angela Krin]] sarcastically describes Bosph trader Mora Bunna|<ref name="Sc">[[Scourge (novel)|''Scourge'']]</ref>}}
The '''Bosphs''' were a [[Mammal/Legends|mammalian]] [[Species/Legends|species]] from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Bosph/Legends|Bosph]] in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]' [[Bosph sector/Legends|Bosph sector]]. They had six [[Limb/Legends|limbs]], no neck and a pair of multiple-lensed [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] that also functioned as [[Ear/Legends|auditory sense organs]]. The Bosphs were a fiercely independent and isolationist species who preferred seclusion from both the rest of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] and each other. The [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]], where most Bosphs remained, was controlled by a hierarchical [[Bosph government|government]] made up of various factions each consisting of [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] Bosphs. These Bosphs were the only individuals who had the right to claim ownership of any object, though any unclaimed object was equally owned by all Bosphs. The Bosphs also kept a tradition of recording the history of their travels, both on Bosph and in the galaxy by [[Tattoo/Legends|tattooing]] [[Star/Legends|star]] [[Star map/Legends|maps]] on to their skin.

Despite their isolation from the galaxy, the Bosphs' homeworld was [[Bombardment of Bosph/Legends|bombarded]] by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] after [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] learned of their Force-sensitive government. The attack devastated the planet's surface and [[Death/Legends|killed]] many Bosphs. Those left alive mostly remained on their homeworld, partially due to a [[Blockade of Bosph|quarantine]] by the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]]. All Bosphs, however, took up a policy of [[Disremembering|disremembrance]] toward the Empire, which was considered the greatest insult a Bosph could give. The attitude meant they no longer acknowledged the existence of the Empire and ignored its laws, believing that the bombardment had actually been punishment from a [[Deity/Legends|deity]]-like entity they called the ''[[Yentarr]]'', meaning the "unknown spirits."

One of the most famous Bosphs in the galaxy was the [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Bora Boru]] who worked during the reign of the Galactic Empire. Another [[Unidentified Bosph bounty hunter|Bosph]] during this period worked as a [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] and answered [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader's]] call to hunt the [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] smuggler and [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and his ship, the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']]. During the reign of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], a Bosph named [[Morga Bunna]] opened a [[Morga Bunna's supply depot|supply depot]] on Bosph's [[Bosph's outermost moon|outermost]] [[Moon/Legends|moon]] after retiring from work as a bounty hunter. Bunna was questioned by the [[Jedi archivist/Legends|Jedi archivist]] [[Mander Zuma]] during the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi's]] quest to shut down the [[Tempest (spice)|Tempest]] [[Spice/Legends|spice]] trade.

==Biology and appearance==
The Bosphs were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] of short<ref name="AE" /> [[Swine|porcine]]<ref name="DitW">{{FFG|story=Dead in the Water|book=Star Wars: Age of Rebellion Game Master's Kit|text=''Star Wars: Age of Rebellion'' Game Master's Kit}}</ref> [[Mammal/Legends|mammals]]<ref name="AE2" /> who generally stood between 1.0 and 1.7 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] tall. The species included at least one [[Gender/Legends|sex]], male.<ref name="SotOR" /> They evolved on the [[Grass/Legends|grassy]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Bosph/Legends|Bosph]] from a [[Bosph precursor species|species]] of six-[[Limb/Legends|limbed]] [[Diet/Legends|omnivores]],<ref name="AE" /> and the Bosph retained the six limbs of their evolutionary ancestors. Bosphs stood upright on two legs and used the remaining four limbs as arms; all six extremities ended in three digits. The species effectively lacked a neck such that a Bosph's head, which was semi-circular in shape, was attached directly to its torso. The species' face consisted of a [[Mouth/Legends|mouth]], and a central, flat, porcine [[Nose/Legends|nose]] between two relatively large [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] on each side of the [[Bone/Legends|skull]].<ref name="SotOR" /> Each eye consisted of hundreds of lenses to allow sight but could also act as a [[tympanic membrane]], which allowed them to act as an [[Ear/Legends|audio sensory organ]].<ref name="AE" /> From behind each eye a single curved horn grew, which was sharp and upward facing.<ref name="SotOR" /> Bosph hide ranged in [[Color/Legends|color]] from light brown to dark grey and was tough and resilient.<ref name="AE" /> The Bosphs' vocal system allowed them to speak in [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Galactic Basic Standard]].<ref name="Sc" />

==Society and culture==
===Isolationist spiritualists===
{{Quote|There is no need to use your warlock ways, Jedi.|Mora Bunna complains of Mander Zuma's attempt to [[Mind trick/Legends|Force persuade]] him|<ref name="Sc" />}}
As a species, Bosphs were fiercely independent and preferred isolation both from other species and other members of their own species,<ref name="AE" /> although there were exceptions who welcomed company.<ref name="Sc" /> Bosphs normally attempted to avoid others as much as possible, and in situations where they did interact with others, they were usually amicable but often indifferent and especially uncaring of what others thought of them. Members of the species were generally deeply philosophical and spiritual in nature; this led their society to seem relatively superstitious, a trait that caused many other species to view them as dull-witted, although this was far from true.<ref name="AE" />

The Bosph species believed in a powerful force known as "the unknown spirits," which they called the ''[[Yentarr]]''—a belief not dissimilar to other beings beliefs in [[Deity/Legends|deities]]. All phenomena that could not be explained were attributed to the ''Yentarr'', with disasters being seen as punishment by them. [[The Force/Legends|The Force]] was one such phenomenon that Bosphs came to believe was caused by the ''Yentarr'', and they named the powers associated with it as ''[[abo b'Yentarr]]'' in their [[Bosph language|language]], meaning "the power of the unknown spirits."<ref name="AE" />

===Led by the Force===
[[File:Bosph-AE.png|thumb|left|200px|Bosphs often had maps tattooed onto their bodies.]]
Bosph society was ruled by a hierarchy of different [[Bosph government|factions]], the members of which were considered ranking officials and were all [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]]. Any Force-sensitive individual, known as ''[[ela b'Yentarr]]''—"the chosen of the unknown spirits"—was apprenticed to one of the factions at a young age and taught the ways of the Force by its members. The exact Force techniques used differed from faction to faction depending upon their role in society. The faction known as the [[gamefinders]] taught their members techniques to help them in [[Hunting/Legends|hunting]], such as [[Force sense/Legends|Force sense]] and [[Precognition/Legends|precognition]], and the [[sickhealers]] learned how to use the Force to heal with techniques like [[Force healing/Legends|Force healing]], [[Control Pain]] and [[Control Disease]].<ref name="AE" />

A faction known as the [[farseers]] also existed who were given access to the teachings of Bosph philosophers.<ref name="SotOR" /> The conduct of each faction also differed, with some having moral codes similar to the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order's]] [[Jedi Code/Legends|code]] and others utilizing the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]], although all ''Ela b'Yentarr'' were aware of the moral implications of using such power. To use the force, Bosphs also believed certain rituals and ceremonies must be performed to summon the unknown spirits, which lead to them not using some longer-lasting force powers.<ref name="AE" />

===Ownership and belongings===
As members of the government factions, ''Ela b'Yentarr'' were also the only individuals in Bosph society granted the right of ownership. All Bosphs believed that any object belonged to all individuals equally unless ownership was claimed of it, and only those with the right of ownership could claim an item and did so using a [[glyph]],<ref name="SotOR" /> a rune-encrusted pendant worn to show the right to ownership. Those with a glyph could claim ownership of any item. However, a conflict of ownership between two or more individuals often resulted in a duel fought for it, sometimes to the [[Death/Legends|death]],<ref name="AE" /> although to kill was not in the Bosphs' nature. Glyphs were also sometimes drawn onto owned objects as well. Due to their unusual view of ownership, many Bosphs who left their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] faced repercussions for stealing the possessions of others that they believed to have claimed as their own.<ref name="SotOR" />

Bosphs generally wore clothes similar to the standard variety found throughout [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] but suited to their particular short, neckless, four-armed bodies. One type of clothing worn by some Bosphs was the [[loincloth]].<ref name="AE2" /><ref name="AE" /><ref name="Topps">{{Topps|set=1993 Topps Star Wars Galaxy Series 1|cardname=114. Mark Nelson|link=https://www.tcdb.com/ViewCard.cfm/sid/75144/cid/5338774/1993-Topps-Star-Wars-Galaxy-114-Mark-Nelson}}</ref> On their feet, some Bosphs wore no shoes at all,<ref name="AE" /> while others wore open-fronted shoes. Some Bosphs left the torso bare and their tattoos more visible.<ref name="AE2" /> A particularly wealthy Bosph might wear golden caps on his horns and high-quality robes.<ref name="Sc" /> At least one member of the farseer faction of Bosphs carried a staff tipped with a [[Crystal/Legends|crystal]] known as a [[Bosph StarCrystal]].<ref name="SotOR" /> Another Bosph had a habit of rocking back and forth while anticipating something and would raise all four of his arms as a sign of resignation. Bosph were capable of snapping their fingers, and members of the species could polish their eyes until they were clean enough to sparkle.<ref name="Sc" />

===Nomadic travelers===
Within early Bosph society, most individuals were solitary nomads and a tradition of tattooing maps and [[Star map/Legends|star maps]] onto their hide evolved to aid them in navigation of the vast grasslands that made up much of their home.<ref name="AE" /> This tradition was maintained in Bosph culture and became known as "the [[way of the traveler]]."<ref name="SotOR" /> The tradition, however, changed slightly and Bosphs who were considered ''Ela b'Yentarr'',<ref name="AE" /> particularly philosophers, always had to bare such tattoos<ref name="SotOR" /> while it was a choice for other Bosphs.<ref name="AE" /> The greatest punishment within Bosph society was 'dis-remembrance' in which the punishers would cease to acknowledge the punished's existence.<ref name="SotOR" />

Some Bosphs who traveled off world brought [[Plant/Legends|plants]] with them and cared for them with [[radiation light]]s.<ref name="Sc" /> Due to the importance of traveling within their society, Bosphs greatly respected members of other species who traveled the stars.<ref name="SotOR" /> In terms of [[Galactic community|galactic society]], Bosphs remained true isolationists and refused a place in any galactic government as well as off-planet [[Merchant/Legends|trade]], although they did use several elements of superior technology from off-world, including [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] technology. Such advanced technology was blended with the Bosphs' own industrial-level technology, which like all things they crafted was suited to use with their four arms. For instance, Bosph [[Music/Legends|musical]] instruments suited their dual pairs of arms; the [[Bosphon Geddy]] was unplayable by any species with less than four arms due to the innate complexities of its design.<ref name="AE" />

==History==
===Evolution and discovery===
{{Quote|Welcome to my humble shop.|Mora Bunna welcomes Mander Zuma and his companions to his supply depot|<ref name="Sc" />}}
[[File:Harsh.png|thumb|right|The Bosph sector was controlled by Moff Harsh during the bombardment of Bosph.]]
The Bosph species evolved from six-limbed omnivores on the planet of Bosph in the [[Bosph system/Legends|Bosph system]]<ref name="AE2" /> of the [[Bosph sector/Legends|Bosph sector]]<ref name="G6">[[Star Wars Gamer 6|''Star Wars Gamer'' 6]]</ref> in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="EAOC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref> The species was forced to evolve due to predation by animals called [[luprisi]], which they had to constantly outsmart leading to their developing sentience. Early Bosphs lived as solitary nomads, tracking animals such as [[umul]]s and [[favria]] that they hunted for meat. To help navigate the huge grasslands they lived in, the Bosph began to tattoo maps onto their hides, something that became a tradition and was maintained in Bosph society, as was the eating of unmuls and favria.<ref name="AE" />

The region of space that Bosph was located in was first explored between [[1000 BBY/Legends|1000 BBY]] and [[25 BBY/Legends|25 BBY]].<ref name="Atlas">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> When the Bosphs were first discovered by off-world [[Scout/Legends|scouts]], the species was offered a place in the galactic government, but declined due to the Bosphs' preference of isolation. While most Bosphs remained on the planet, some chose to leave and explore the galaxy, tattooing [[Realspace/Legends|space]] charts on their hide to maintain tradition, with some Bosphs exploring so widely that their tattoos showed [[Star system/Legends|star systems]] not even discovered by professional scouts.<ref name="AE" />

===Destruction of Bosph===
About a [[Standard year/Legends|year]] after the start of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] appointed the [[Human/Legends|Human]] Imperial [[Officer/Legends|officer]] [[Harsh]] as [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] of the Bosph [[Sector/Legends|sector]] due to the man's success in the [[Battle of Chabosh (Galactic Civil War)|Battle of Chabosh]] at the start of the war, where he had defeated a rebellion on the [[Chabosh|planet]]. During Harsh's tenure,<ref name="G6" /> the [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]]<ref name="AE" />—a Force-sensitive himself<ref name="RotJ">{{Film|VI}}</ref>—became aware of the Force-sensitive factions of Bosphs. The Empire immediately [[Blockade of Bosph|quarantined]] the planet, and shortly afterwards,<ref name="AE" /> [[Bombardment of Bosph/Legends|bombarded]] the surface of the planet from [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]], killing huge numbers of Bosphs and decimating the planet's grasslands.<ref name="SotOR" /> Of the few Bosphs that survived, most remained on their homeworld, although a few escaped and slipped through the quarantine on any [[Starship/Legends|ships]] available.<ref name="AE" />

Despite the planet's relative obscurity due to its inhabitants' self-exile from galactic society, some off-worlders were aware of its destruction, although none knew of the cause. Rumors spread that it had been due to the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] setting up on the planet, or as part of a personal vendetta against Bosph "royalty." However, few other species cared about the bombardment, due to the small number of Bosphs left.<ref name="SotOR" /> The Bosphs themselves came to believe the attack had been a religious purge and adopted a policy of [[Disremembering|dis-remembrance]] toward the Empire, refusing to acknowledge its existence and ignoring its laws and blockade of their planet. They attributed the destruction of the bombardment instead to the ''Yentarr'' and saw it as a punishment for some misdeed of their species.<ref name="AE" /> By [[19 ABY/Legends|19 ABY]], a conflict had destroyed much of Bosph's [[Bosph's outermost moon|outermost]] [[Moon/Legends|moon]], although what remained had been made into a [[Morga Bunna's supply depot|supply depot]] by a retired Bosph [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] named [[Morga Bunna]].<ref name="Sc" />

==Bosph in the galaxy==
===Scouts and bounty hunters===
{{Quote|I welcomed them, of course, much like I welcome you. It gets lonely out here in space, and any company is welcome.|Mora Bunna, a rare exception to Bosph's preference to isolation|<ref name="Sc" />}}
[[File:Bosph_hunter.jpg|thumb|left|Some Bosphs took up work as bounty hunters.]]
Few Bosphs left their homeworld due to their isolationist tendencies and the Imperial blockade on Bosph during the reign of the Galactic Empire. The majority of Bosphs found off-world were refugees who fled the Imperial bombardment, although a small number of Bosphs embraced off-world technologies and took up galactic travel by choice. Those who did join galactic society for whatever reason generally took up jobs that provided the isolation from others that they enjoyed.<ref name="AE" />

At least two Bosphs became bounty hunters, including one [[Unidentified Bosph bounty hunter|individual]] who answered the call of the Sith Lord [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Vader]], the [[Sith apprentice/Legends|apprentice]] of Emperor Palpatine, when he sought out hunters to capture the [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], who was helping the Rebel Alliance.<ref name="Topps" /> Another Bosph named Morga Bunna worked as a bounty hunter as well, but by 19 ABY had retired to run a storage depot on the outermost moon of Bosph. His depot was used by many clients to store goods that were dangerous to hold onto or to perform interactions with individuals they would prefer not to meet face to face. The Human [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Jedi archivist/Legends|archivist]] [[Mander Zuma]] and his companions visited Bunna's outpost during their crackdown on the [[Tempest (spice)|Tempest]] [[Spice/Legends|spice]] trade across [[Hutt Space/Legends|Hutt Space]] and the [[Corporate Sector/Legends|Corporate Sector]].<ref name="Sc" />

Bunna's store of Tempest had been withdrawn, and the trader refused to give up his clients to the Jedi. He stated that he did not have any details since most of his business was done through blind drops—although he did give the visitors a [[Data cube/Legends|data cube]] recording each anonymous transaction. Upon the arrival of the [[Invincible-class Dreadnaught Heavy Cruiser|''Invincible''-class Dreadnaught Heavy Cruiser]] [[Resolute (Invincible-class)|''Resolute'']] at the depot, Bunna maintained that he had no information for the Jedi but agreed to help them find the head of the [[Bomu Clan]] of [[Hutt/Legends|Hutts]]. The [[Corporate Sector Authority/Legends|Corporate Sector Authority]] confiscated several items in his inventory stolen from Corporate Sector citizens before leaving the depot. Unlike most Bosphs, Mora, claimed to enjoy the company of other sentient beings.<ref name="Sc" />

===Bora Boru===
[[File:Bora_Boru.jpg|thumb|right|upright|The Bosph smuggler Bora Boru]]
Another Bosph, [[Bora Boru]], became a smuggler during the reign of the Empire. Originally part of the farseer faction of Bosphs, Boru survived his homeworld's bombardment by the Empire and came to hate them for it as they had denied him the opportunity to die with his people. He, like most surviving Bosphs, followed a policy of dis-remembrance of the Empire, believing that seeking revenge would be suicidal. As a member of a government faction, Boru was both Force-sensitive and had the right of ownership, along with the appropriate glyph pendant.<ref name="SotOR" />

Using the glyph, he claimed ownership of the vessel ''[[Bosphon Forever]]'', a [[Surronian (species)|Surronian]] [[Surronian L19 heavy freighter/Legends|L19 freighter]], and killed its [[Owner of the Bosphon Forever|owner]] when the man tried to reclaim it. The smuggler went on to take up a successful career in smuggling, often aiding those who opposed or hated the Empire. Due to his Force abilities, he lived longer than the average for his species, and as such had far more heavily tattooed skin than most Bosphs as well as a high ability to astrogate and an expansive knowledge of the star systems of the galaxy.<ref name="SotOR" />

===Tay Coomsay===
{{Quote|This be ''Shadow Raptor''. I Tay Coomsay. Come bridge.|Tay Coomsay spoke in broken basic|<ref name="DitW" />}}
During the Galactic Civil War, the Bosph male [[Tay Coomsay]] served as a [[Navigator/Legends|navigator]] on board the ''[[Shadow Raptor]]'', an [[Alliance Intelligence/Legends|Alliance Intelligence]] [[EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate/Legends|EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate]] operating throughout the [[Trans-Hydian/Legends|Trans-Hydian Borderlands]]. He spoke in monosyllabic broken Basic and wore the traditional Bosph star map tattoos on his arms, becoming offended if anyone suggested that they were a security risk.<ref name="DitW" />

{{RPG}}
While the ''Shadow Raptor'' was in the [[Listehol system/Legends|Listehol system]], the transport ''[[Spinster's Loom]]'' docked with the [[Frigate/Legends|frigate]] to deliver a group of Alliance agents. Coomsay greeted the agents at the frigate's [[Airlock/Legends|airlock]] and led them to the ship's [[Bridge (starship)|bridge]] while giving a brief tour. On the bridge, the agents were assigned a mission to collect a stolen shipment of [[Droid/Legends|droids]] from the [[Port Tooga]] [[Space station/Legends|space station]]. After the group successfully returned to the ''Shadow Raptor'' with the droids, the frigate jumped to [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]]; however, unbeknownst to the rebels, the shipment of droids actually served [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Imperial Intelligence]] and began [[Shadow Raptor revolt|attacking]] the ship and its crew. After the Imperial droids managed to secure the bridge, Coomsay and [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Hylo Sortuli]] were taken captive and led to the frigate's aft [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]] bay by [[TJ-11]], a [[T-series tactical droid/Legends|T-series tactical droid]] who intended to escape with the pair and deliver them to its Imperial masters. Before TJ-11 could escape, the agents who had delivered the droids managed to defeat it, rescue Coomsay and Sortuli, and retake the ''Shadow Raptor''.<ref name="DitW" />
{{Endgame}}

==Behind the scenes==
The Bosphs were created as part of [[Topps]]' ''[[Star Wars]]'' trading cards, appearing in ''[[1993 Topps Star Wars Galaxy Series 1]]'' released in [[1993]]. On the card, a Bosph bounty hunter was depicted meeting with Darth Vader alongside other hunters, including [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]], in an illustration by [[Mark Nelson]]. The species, however, was not named and no information was given on it.<ref name="Topps" />

The species itself was then named and expanded upon for [[West End Games]]' [[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game, Second Edition|''Star Wars'' roleplaying game]], with the character Bora Boru appearing in the [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 5|fifth]] issue of the ''[[Star Wars Adventure Journal]]'', in an article titled "[[Smugglers of the Outer Rim]]" written by [[Doug Shuler]] and published in [[1995]].<ref name="SotOR" /> A Bosph then appeared for the first time in the [[2012]] novel [[Scourge (novel)|''Scourge'']] by [[Jeff Grubb]].<ref name="Sc" />
